verses.text: At Capernaum so many gather that there is no room. Drawing power. The word of Christ packs the house.
At Capernaum so many gather that there is no room. Drawing power. The word of Christ packs the house.
Four lower a palsied man through the roof; 'when Jesus saw their faith,' He says, 'thy sins be forgiven thee.' Bold faith. He answers the deepest need first.
Four lower a palsied man through the roof; 'when Jesus saw their faith,' He says, 'thy sins be forgiven thee.' Bold faith. He answers the deepest need first.
Four lower a palsied man through the roof; 'when Jesus saw their faith,' He says, 'thy sins be forgiven thee.' Bold faith. He answers the deepest need first.
The scribes think, Who can forgive but God? He asks which is easier, to forgive or to heal. Divine claim. He does what only God can do.
The scribes think, Who can forgive but God? He asks which is easier, to forgive or to heal. Divine claim. He does what only God can do.
The scribes think, Who can forgive but God? He asks which is easier, to forgive or to heal. Divine claim. He does what only God can do.
The scribes think, Who can forgive but God? He asks which is easier, to forgive or to heal. Divine claim. He does what only God can do.
'That ye may know the Son of man hath power... to forgive sins,' He bids the man arise; he does, and all are amazed. Proof given. The cure confirms the pardon.
'That ye may know the Son of man hath power... to forgive sins,' He bids the man arise; he does, and all are amazed. Proof given. The cure confirms the pardon.
'That ye may know the Son of man hath power... to forgive sins,' He bids the man arise; he does, and all are amazed. Proof given. The cure confirms the pardon.
By the sea He calls Levi the publican, 'Follow me'; and he rises and follows. Grace to a tax-man. The despised are not beyond Christ's call.
By the sea He calls Levi the publican, 'Follow me'; and he rises and follows. Grace to a tax-man. The despised are not beyond Christ's call.
Eating with sinners, He says, 'they that are whole have no need of the physician'; 'I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.' The Physician. He seeks those who own their need.
Eating with sinners, He says, 'they that are whole have no need of the physician'; 'I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.' The Physician. He seeks those who own their need.
Eating with sinners, He says, 'they that are whole have no need of the physician'; 'I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.' The Physician. He seeks those who own their need.
Asked of fasting, He says the bridechamber children cannot fast while the bridegroom is present. A time for joy. His presence turns mourning into gladness.
Asked of fasting, He says the bridechamber children cannot fast while the bridegroom is present. A time for joy. His presence turns mourning into gladness.
Asked of fasting, He says the bridechamber children cannot fast while the bridegroom is present. A time for joy. His presence turns mourning into gladness.
No man sews new cloth on old, nor new wine in old bottles. Something new. The gospel is no patch on the old order.
No man sews new cloth on old, nor new wine in old bottles. Something new. The gospel is no patch on the old order.
His disciples pluck corn on the sabbath; He cites David eating the shewbread. Mercy over rule. Need and kindness are no breach of God's day.
His disciples pluck corn on the sabbath; He cites David eating the shewbread. Mercy over rule. Need and kindness are no breach of God's day.
His disciples pluck corn on the sabbath; He cites David eating the shewbread. Mercy over rule. Need and kindness are no breach of God's day.
His disciples pluck corn on the sabbath; He cites David eating the shewbread. Mercy over rule. Need and kindness are no breach of God's day.
'The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath'; the Son of man is its Lord. For our good. The day is a gift, and Christ is its Master.
'The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath'; the Son of man is its Lord. For our good. The day is a gift, and Christ is its Master.
